text: When you write code, you will often find bugs or errors. Identifying bugs is the first step. Look at what the program does wrong and where it stops working. Use debugging tools like a breakpoint or a step-through debugger. These tools let you pause the program and check values. A systematic approach is very helpful. Start from the error and trace backward. Check one small part at a time. Do not change many things at once. Also, use logging. Print messages to the console to see what your program is doing. This helps you follow the flow and find hidden problems. With practice, debugging becomes much faster.
